claims.title: ACME worker queues enforce bounded backpressure
claims.description: The ACME worker fallback queue is bounded, supports timeout-aware producer backpressure and consumer idle behavior, exposes queue depth, and rejects new work after closure while draining existing work.

claims.title: Canonical AppSpec corpus preserves REST and JSON-RPC parity
claims.description: Canonical AppSpec corpus cases materialize and execute under uvicorn with stable REST/JSON-RPC parity behavior.

claims.title: Negative AppSpec corpus lanes fail closed
claims.description: Negative AppSpec corpus cases preserve fail-closed runtime behavior for unsupported dispatch paths.

claims.title: AppSpec and RouterSpec nested composition remains typed
claims.description: AppSpec accepts RouterSpec instances whose router-owned tables carry model_ref, response, operations, storage, field, IO, and foreign-key metadata without unresolved string surfaces.

claims.title: AuthN provider integration status-code closure
claims.description: The AuthN provider integration preserves governed status-code behavior: missing credentials on required provider auth return 403 and wrong bearer credentials return 401, while direct HTTPBearer challenge behavior remains separately verified.

claims.title: BindingSpec KernelPlan protocol compilation target contract
claims.description: BindingSpec protocol metadata compiles into deterministic KernelPlan inputs with governed family, framing, atom anchors, lifecycle rows, and fail-closed unsupported binding validation.

claims.title: Boundary-scoped test selection is deterministic.
claims.description: Boundary and release inputs resolve to stable sorted feature, claim, test, and evidence manifests.

claims.title: Compiled loop regions target contract
claims.description: Loop regions compile into phase-tree structures with continue, exit, close, completion fence, err edge, ErrorCtx metadata, and fail-closed validation.

claims.title: Default root path contract is covered by Python and Rust tests
claims.description: The default root path contract has explicit Python unit and Rust runtime contract coverage linked through SSOT evidence rows.

claims.title: OpenAPI, OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, and JSON Schema read from canonical metadata sources.
claims.description: OpenAPI consumes route-facing metadata from Route plus bindings/models/schemas, while OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, and JSON Schema continue to derive from their canonical op, binding, and schema inputs without a docs-owned route registry.

claims.title: Documentation endpoints mount as ordinary routes and normalized route-backed ops.
claims.description: Documentation and system endpoints are mounted as ordinary imperative routes, set inherit_owner_dependencies=False where required, and normalize into the same __tigrbl_route_ops__ carrier without any surface_kind abstraction.

claims.title: External persistence error details are not exposed
claims.description: HTTP and JSON-RPC transport error responses sanitize persistence-layer exceptions and do not expose ORM, DBAPI, SQL text, table names, or bound parameters externally.

claims.title: Factories are canonical and shortcuts re-export factory aliases.
claims.description: The canonical construction surface lives under factories for make*/define*/derive* helpers and direct factory aliases such as acol, vcol, and op, while shortcuts remains a thin re-export layer.

claims.title: Every SSOT in-scope feature has verification coverage or a governance validator rationale.
claims.description: Feature rows link to tests; governance-only rows can be covered by the SSOT authority validator.

claims.title: First-class callback runtime target contract
claims.description: Callback runtime surfaces callbacks as descriptor metadata, strips callables from KernelPlan metadata, executes callback fences in order, fails closed for missing callbacks, wraps callback exceptions with phase metadata, and roundtrips descriptors through the runtime codec.

claims.title: First-class webhook delivery target contract
claims.description: Webhook delivery compiles explicit event endpoint signing retry metadata, signs deterministic idempotent payloads, records emit-complete on success, retries transient failures, dead-letters terminal failures, and preserves timeout ErrorCtx without completion.

claims.title: HTTP REST and JSON-RPC atom chains target contract
claims.description: HTTP REST and JSON-RPC unary chains compile canonical atom order, shared operation semantics, framing decode/encode, completion-fence behavior, and fail-closed unsupported binding validation.

claims.title: HTTP routes are concrete op aliases with HttpRestBindingSpec bindings.
claims.description: Binding-first REST decorators materialize concrete custom ops through op_ctx(bindings=(HttpRestBindingSpec(...), ...)), and imperative HTTP routes normalize equivalent route-backed ops into the owner-local __tigrbl_route_ops__ spec carrier.

claims.title: System document HTTP routes use the same concrete registration path as user routes.
claims.description: OpenAPI, OpenRPC, AsyncAPI, JSON Schema, Swagger, Lens, favicon, and diagnostics endpoints mount as ordinary routes and normalize into the same route-backed op carrier as user-defined imperative routes.

claims.title: Deprecated tigrbl_canon HTTP route registration surfaces are removed from the active line.
claims.description: The active line exposes no separate HTTP route registration subsystem: module-level route_ctx(...) and route(...) are removed, register_http_route(...) is absent, and route normalization happens through ordinary decorators and imperative route ownership.

claims.title: HTTP stream atom chains target contract
claims.description: HTTP stream atom chains compile iterator, chunk emission, disconnect finalization, and POST_EMIT completion-fence behavior.

claims.title: Lifespan runtime chain target contract
claims.description: Lifespan runtime chains compile KernelPlan-owned startup and shutdown atom order, set readiness only after successful startup, clear readiness on shutdown, and report typed handler failure ErrorCtx metadata.

claims.title: DataTypeSpec and StorageTypeRef define the active canonical semantic datatype center for the next target.
ColumnSpec.datatype is the canonical datatype attachment point on the active next-target line.
TypeAdapter, BaseTypeAdapter, and TypeRegistry provide the executable datatype contract on the active next-target line.
EngineTypeLowerer, EngineRegistry, and EngineDatatypeBridge control engine-owned lowering on the active next-target line.
ReflectedTypeMapper governs reverse mapping and reflection import behavior on the active next-target line.
Relational, dataframe, file, cache, and probabilistic engine families align to the datatype bridge contract on the active next-target line.
Table portability and interoperability rules are specified as governed next-target work on the active line.
Reflection-driven round-trip schema recovery is defined with best-effort and metadata-preserving modes on the active next-target line.

claims.title: Gunicorn is part of the governed supported server set.
claims.description: Gunicorn remains inside the governed first-class server set for the unified CLI server-selection contract.

claims.title: Hypercorn is part of the governed supported server set.
claims.description: Hypercorn remains inside the governed first-class server set for the unified CLI server-selection contract.

claims.title: Tigrcorn is part of the governed supported server set.
claims.description: Tigrcorn remains inside the governed first-class server set for the unified CLI server-selection contract.

claims.title: Uvicorn is part of the governed supported server set.
claims.description: Uvicorn remains inside the governed first-class server set for the unified CLI server-selection contract.

claims.title: SessionSpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: SessionSpec supports alias normalization, scoped merge precedence, adapter kwargs, helper constructors, factory validation, and serde roundtrip for consistency, tenancy, cache, compliance, pagination, and observability metadata.

claims.title: SPIFFE HTTPX clients use helper TLS context
claims.description: The SPIFFE HTTPX client helper must pass the TLS helper SSL context into httpx.AsyncClient so outbound HTTPS clients honor configured SPIFFE TLS material.

claims.title: SPIFFE SVID structural validation is fail closed
claims.description: SvidValidator rejects malformed X.509 and JWT SVID material, JWT alg none, expired tokens, premature tokens, and audience mismatches without claiming full cryptographic chain validation.

claims.title: SseBindingSpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: SseBindingSpec preserves server-stream exchange, GET method contract, and SSE framing through serde.

claims.title: SSE session message stream chains target contract
claims.description: SSE chains declare session open/close, stream start/end, message emission, heartbeat emission, disconnect handling, and POST_EMIT completion.

claims.title: SSOT is authoritative over docs and CI projections.
claims.description: SSOT registry, ADR, and SPEC records define governed truth; docs and CI are projections and guardrails.

claims.title: Static file runtime chain target contract
claims.description: Static file runtime chains resolve mounted files, reject traversal before lookup, return governed 404s, compile range/cache/pathsend atoms, and emit partial-content metadata.

claims.title: Status synchronization is auditable and reproducible.
claims.description: Status changes are derived from machine-readable test results and can be emitted as dry-run audit artifacts before registry mutation.

claims.title: StorageTransformSpec roundtrips named storage transforms
claims.description: StorageTransformSpec preserves named to_stored and from_stored callables across serde boundaries for storage-boundary value transforms.

claims.title: StorageTypeRef preserves engine physical type references
claims.description: StorageTypeRef preserves engine_kind and physical_name, participates in datatype lowering/reflection, and is available through public tigrbl exports.

claims.title: TableRegistrySpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: TableRegistrySpec preserves default empty registries, declared table ordering, public facade export, and serde roundtrip behavior.

claims.title: TemplateSpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: TemplateSpec preserves declarative template metadata, ResponseSpec embedding, serde roundtrip behavior, and response-resolution merge semantics.

claims.title: Tigrbl 0.3.19 declared surface docs extension is implemented
claims.description: The declared-surface docs extension is in the 0.3.19 certification boundary and has passing unit coverage.

claims.title: Tigrbl 0.3.19 pre-TX security dependency is implemented
claims.description: The pre-TX security dependency execution surface is in the 0.3.19 certification boundary and has passing unit coverage.

claims.title: tigrbl_concrete does not import tigrbl_kernel
claims.description: tigrbl_concrete does not statically or dynamically import tigrbl_kernel, does not declare tigrbl-kernel as a dependency, and package import does not load tigrbl_kernel; only tigrbl_runtime owns kernel integration.

claims.title: tigrbl_tests failing cases are closed
claims.description: Asserts that every pytest case failing in the tigrbl_tests closure sweep is now passing under the full tigrbl_tests run, with the final JUnit and pytest output attached as evidence.

claims.title: Tools, tests, evidence, claims, and gates have separate SSOT roles.
claims.description: Tools execute; tests verify; evidence records proof; claims assert proof meaning; gates aggregate release decisions.

claims.title: REST and JSON-RPC parity remains the governed semantic contract for the shared transport-dispatch path.
claims.description: The transport-dispatch track preserves REST and JSON-RPC parity as a semantic contract and rejects test-only relaxation as the repair strategy.

claims.title: Tree and loop aware executors target contract
claims.description: Loop-aware executors honor continuation, break, close, disconnect, loop-local errors, and ErrorCtx routing without flattening phase-tree semantics.

claims.title: Two-axis lifecycle matrix target contract
claims.description: Lifecycle compilation uses runtime family and subevent rows against kernel phase columns, with cells carrying participation, hookability, tx, loop, and legality metadata.

claims.title: WebSocket and WSS atom chains target contract
claims.description: WebSocket/WSS chains compile connect, accept, receive loop, decode, handler, send, close, close metadata, and WSS scope metadata behavior.

claims.title: WebTransportBindingSpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: WebTransportBindingSpec preserves webtransport protocol, bidirectional exchange, and webtransport framing through serde.

claims.title: WsBindingSpec contract is implemented and tested
claims.description: WsBindingSpec preserves ws/wss protocol metadata, subprotocols, bidirectional exchange, and text framing through serde.
